chunks.section: Sathak Innovation and Incubation Foundation (SIIF) — Overview & Incubation Cell
chunks.context: Describes the founding objects of SIIF and the services offered by the Incubation Cell including infrastructure, funding, mentorship, and events.
chunks.text:
Sathak Innovation and Incubation Foundation (SIIF) — Main Objects:

a) To promote commerce, science, education and research by helping, establishing and incubating startups, individuals, teams with innovative ideas to make prototypes and convert them into products.
b) To promote a culture of innovation-driven entrepreneurship in the fields of science, engineering, technology, commerce, agriculture, health care, medicine, pharmacy, education, arts, sports and other various fields.
c) To establish, help establish and/or manage infrastructure, laboratories, to make and test prototypes/products, pilot plant production facilities, software development facilities, training and human resource development centres, propagate new scientific and technological development and innovation from idea stage to full-scale commercial production or any part of this chain. For the above purposes to assist, co-operate or collaborate with any establishment, laboratory, technology business incubators centre, organisation or institution in India or abroad.

Incubation Cell — Services Offered:
- Physical office space: approx. 5000 sq.ft.
- Event halls: 100-seater, 400-seater, 700-seater halls for Entrepreneurship events.
- Funding opportunities: grants, seed funding, and connections to potential investors.
- Mentorship programs: experienced professionals and industry experts provide guidance.
- Access to network of investors, venture capitalists, and angel investors.
- Workshops, training programs, and events for innovation and entrepreneurial skills.
- Partnerships with academic institutions, industry bodies, and government agencies.

Incubation Lead:
Name | Role | Organisation | Email | Website
Akshita K | Incubation Lead | SIIF, MSAJCE — Mohamed Sathak A.J. College of Engineering, Chennai - 603103 | info@siif.ventures | www.siif.ventures

chunks.section: National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) — Vision, Mission & Objectives
chunks.context: Covers the NISP vision and mission statements and both short-term and long-term objectives for promoting innovation and entrepreneurship at MSAJCE.
chunks.text:
National Innovation and Startup Policy (NISP) — MSAJCE

Vision:
To cater the needs of student entrepreneurs with innovative ideas thereby introducing a culture of entrepreneurship inside campus which will strengthen our education system and thereby promoting national economic and social growth.

Mission:
To develop an ecosystem with required infrastructure that can enable students and faculty to innovate and prototype their potential ideas with industrial standards and support from Government, industry and reputed academic institutions around the world and help them to realize their potentials.

Short Term Objectives:
1. To help student groups to prototype their ideas.
2. To improve innovation, creative and design thinking among student community.
3. Incubation facility for faculty driven start-up and student/alumni start-up.
4. Organize FDP, seminars and workshops, distinguish talks for students, faculty and alumni and promote entrepreneurial culture.
5. Strengthen institute-industry interaction cell activity and effectively use the outcomes for achieving the mission.

Long Term Objectives:
1. Associate with DST, CII, MSME and other academic institutions for transferring world class facility for MSAJCE IEDC activities.
2. Improve quality of research work among students and to attain patent, which can be commercially used in production.
3. Provide a platform for students to develop innovative products with global recognition and generate business opportunities.
4. Generate revenues through consultancy work and student start-ups.
5. Spread awareness to students and faculty regarding IPR related activities.
6. Strategic partnership linkage with:
   a. Entrepreneurship Development Institute of India (EDII), Chennai.
   b. National Innovation Foundation (NIF) of India to submit ideas and apply for schemes.
   c. Procure fund from AICTE for Entrepreneurship Development Cell.

chunks.section: KPI Monitor & Evaluation — Innovation Outcomes and Activities
chunks.context: Detailed KPI framework for MSAJCE's innovation and startup policy covering vision goals, outcomes, and planned activities with verification mechanisms.
chunks.text:
KPI Monitor & Evaluation:

Hierarchy | Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) | Verification
Vision | Increase in Self-Employment Rate; 5 Established Start-ups per year | NIRF Rankings
Goal | Establish Environment with multiple levels of support for innovation & Entrepreneurship and startups; Students will choose Entrepreneurship as career | ARIIA Rankings
Outcomes | All students & faculty with entrepreneurship orientation; Students & faculty motivated to start entrepreneurial activity; 5 IPR/Innovations developed ideas/year; 5 Student/Early-Stage Start-ups to be formed/year; In-house Expert Capacity facility available for Advisory Services; 25% of Student & faculty mass exposed to awareness/orientation programs; Students covered through entrepreneurship Education via MOOC, Classroom, Experiential Learning; 250 beneficiaries accessing infrastructure & facilities per day/month/year; 25% innovators identified, 25 awarded/recognized, 25 supported; 5% Student projects turn to commercialised innovations; 5 IPR-based product/services generated and registration filed; In-house trained professional developed for advisory services; 5 Research Studies on Entrepreneurship published; 2 Regional/National/International linkages established for start-up & innovation; 5% Representatives of experts & entrepreneurial students across Dept. & Disciplines; 5 Beneficiaries referred to Incubators/investors; 10 Beneficiaries generated under various schemes | Quarterly Newsletter, Biannual Survey, Monthly Progress Report
Activities | 5 Education/Skill certification programs on Entrepreneurship, IPR, Innovation; 25 workshops, awareness, market outreach events; 12 networking events (Intra and Inter-institutional) organized; 5 skill and competency development training programs/FDPs/EDPs; 5 research studies on Entrepreneurship; 1 national/regional award and campus Hackathon; Incentivizing Entrepreneurship with policies, toolkits, Start-up Manual; 1% of total budget/year against total institution revenue for start-up | Biannual Survey, Quarterly Newsletter, Monthly Progress Report, Review Meetings

chunks.section: Tentative 5-Year Activity Plan & Entrepreneurship Development Cell (EDC)
chunks.context: Lists all 20 planned activities over 5 years for innovation and startup development, and describes the roles and key members of the Entrepreneurship Development Cell.
chunks.text:
Tentative 5-Year Activity Plan:

S.No | Activity | Frequency
1 | One Day Workshop on Entrepreneurship and Innovation as Career Opportunity | One per Year
2 | One Day Workshop on Problem Solving/Design Thinking/Ideation/Campus Hackathon | One per Year
3 | Field/Exposure Visit to Village/Society/School/Industry/Market — Identify Real Life Problems | One per Year

6 | National Conference/Workshop on Start-up/Social Innovation & Entrepreneurship | One per Year
7 | Demo Day – Exhibition Cum Demo for PoCs & Mentorship Session for Innovators/Student Entrepreneurs | Two per Year
8 | Internship at Innovation & Start-up Centre/Startups/Incubation Unit during Semester Break | One per Year
9 | Field/Exposure Visit to Incubation Unit/Patent Facilitation Centre/Technology Transfer Centre | One per Year
10 | Business Plan Contest | One per Year
11 | One Day Awareness/Mentoring Session on IPR & IP Management for Innovation and Start-ups | One per Year

15 | Boot Camp for Innovation Product Development | One per Year
16 | Innovation Day Celebrations | One per Year
17 | National Science Day | One per Year
18 | Workshop on Funding Opportunities for Innovation and Entrepreneurship Development | One per Year
19 | SATHAKATHON — A National Level 24-Hour Hackathon | One per Year
20 | Short Term Training Course on Innovation/Start-up & Entrepreneurship | One per Year

Entrepreneurship Development Cell (EDC) — Roles and Responsibilities:
1. Encourage students to become entrepreneurs by organizing entrepreneurship development programs and making them participate in activities organized by external agencies.
2. Organize training for Startup/Incubator and arrange mentoring to proceed further.
3. Coordinate with Strategy Partner of Sathak Innovation Incubation Cell (SIIF) and execute various activities.

EDC Key Members:
Mr. S.V. Vinodh / EEE — Head EDC & HEC
Mrs. N. Kavitha / MBA
Mr. Ajin Sijo John / MECH
Mrs. N. Selvi / Training

chunks.section: Institution Innovation Council (IIC) — About, Vision, Mission & Office Bearers
chunks.context: Covers the IIC constitution, its major functions, vision, mission, and office bearer details for MSAJCE's Institution Innovation Council.
chunks.text:
Institution Innovation Council (IIC) — MSAJCE

IIC-MSAJCE has been constituted as per guidelines of the Ministry of Human Resource Development Innovation Cell (MIC).

Introduction:
Ministry of Human Resource Development (MHRD), Govt. of India has established 'MHRD's Innovation Cell (MIC)' to systematically foster the culture of innovation amongst all Higher Education Institutions (HEIs). The primary mandate of MIC is to encourage, inspire and nurture young students by supporting them to work with new ideas and transform them into prototypes while they are in their formative years.

MIC has envisioned encouraging creation of 'Institution's Innovation Councils (IICs)' across selected HEIs. A network of IICs is established to promote innovation in institutions through multitudinous modes leading to an innovation promotion eco-system in campuses.

Major Focus of IIC:
- To create a vibrant local innovation ecosystem.
- Start-up supporting mechanism in HEIs.
- Prepare institute for Atal Ranking of Institutions on Innovation Achievements (ARIIA) Framework.
- Establish Function at Ecosystem for Scouting Ideas and Pre-incubation of Ideas.
- Develop better Cognitive Ability for Technology Students.

Functions of IIC:
- Identify and reward innovations and share success stories.
- Organize periodic workshops/seminars/interactions with entrepreneurs, investors, professionals and create a mentor pool for student innovators.
- Network with peers and national entrepreneurship development organizations.
- Create an Institution's Innovation Portal to highlight innovative projects by institution's faculty and students.
- Organize Hackathons, idea competitions, mini-challenges with industry involvement.

IIC Vision:
To be a centre of excellence for transforming students into proficient Electrical and Electronics Engineers through sustainable practices.

IIC Mission:
M1. Impart core fundamental knowledge and necessary skills in Electrical and Electronics Engineering through innovative teaching and learning methodology.
M2. Inculcate critical thinking, ethics, lifelong learning and creativity needed for industry and society.
M3. Cultivate students with all-round competencies for career, higher education and self-employability.

IIC Office Bearers:
Role | Name | Designation
President | Mr. S. Vimalathithan | Associate Professor & NISP Coordinator
Convenor | Dr. Ramya | —
Student-Convenor | Mr. Yogesh R | B.Tech - IT (2022–2026)

IIC Contact:

chunks.overlap_text: IIC conducted 24 events in 2022–2023 across departments including EEE, MECH, CSE, IT, ECE, CIVIL. Largest event had 300 student beneficiaries (Workshop on Entrepreneurship and Innovation, Nov 2022, IT dept). KAPILA Kalam Program saw 218 student participants.
